title: More Nonsense Limerick 18
content:
There was an old man in a Marsh,
    Whose manners were futile and harsh;
    He sate on a log, 
    And sang songs to a frog,
    That instructive old man in a Marsh.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Lear, titled "More Nonsense Limerick 18", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
